---
title: "Video Game Sales Extended Analysis (Extended)"
author: "Mark Hamer"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

# Load and clean data
```{r}
games <- read.csv("video_games.csv")
games <- as_tibble(games)
games_clean <- games %>% drop_na()
```

## Global Sales by Genre (ORIGINAL)
```{r}
genre_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Genre) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_sales))
ggplot(genre_sales, aes(x = reorder(Genre, total_sales), y = total_sales)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Global Sales by Genre", x = "Genre", y = "Sales")
```

## Global Sales by Platform (ORIGINAL)
```{r}
platform_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Platform) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_sales))
ggplot(platform_sales, aes(x = reorder(Platform, total_sales), y = total_sales)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Global Sales by Platform", x = "Platform", y = "Sales")
```

## Sales Over Time (ORIGINAL)
```{r}
year_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales))
ggplot(year_sales, aes(x = Year, y = total_sales)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Sales Over Time", x = "Year", y = "Global Sales")
```

## Top 10 Publishers by Global Sales (EXTENDED)
```{r}
publisher_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Publisher) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_sales)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)
ggplot(publisher_sales, aes(x = reorder(Publisher, total_sales), y = total_sales)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Publishers by Global Sales",
       x = "Publisher", y = "Global Sales (millions)")
```

## Regional Sales Breakdown (EXTENDED)
```{r}
games_clean %>%
  summarise(NA_Sales = sum(NA_Sales), EU_Sales = sum(EU_Sales),
            JP_Sales = sum(JP_Sales), Other_Sales = sum(Other_Sales)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Region", values_to = "Sales") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Region, y = Sales, fill = Region)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Sales by Region", y = "Sales (millions)")
```
